title: Mathematics + Financial Literacy: ICSE Class 8 — Profit, Loss and Interest
subject: Mathematics

questions.text: Ramesh buys a laptop for ₹35,000 and sells it for ₹40,250. What is his profit percentage?
questions.explanation: Profit = 40250 - 35000 = ₹5,250. Profit% = (5250/35000)×100 = 15%. Tech product resellers in India typically aim for 10-20% margin.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.text: A bank offers compound interest at 10% per annum. If Sita deposits ₹4,000, what is the amount after 2 years?
questions.explanation: A = P(1 + r/100)^t = 4000(1.1)^2 = 4000×1.21 = ₹4,840. Compare to SI: 4000+(4000×10×2/100) = ₹4,800. CI gives ₹40 more — this gap grows enormously over longer periods!
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.text: A jacket is marked ₹2,000 with 20% discount. 18% GST is applied on the discounted price. What is the final price?
questions.explanation: Discounted price = 2000 × 0.8 = ₹1,600. GST = 18% of 1600 = ₹288. Final price = 1600+288 = ₹1,888. This is exactly how India's GST works — discount first, then tax on discounted price.
questions.subject: Mathematics
